stories.title: Norway’s pro-EU voices sense their moment
stories.summary: OSLO — In a suburban restaurant outside the Norwegian capital, a handful of pro-Europe activists are trying to do something that has failed for decades: drag their country back into the EU debate. Now, they may finally have an opening. But the catalyst for these discussions isn’t in Oslo. It’s in Reykjavik. In August, Iceland will hold a referendum to decide on new accession negotiations with the EU, offering Norway a chance to put the issue back on its political agenda. “We have this summer to

stories.title: Six people found dead inside train car at rail yard near Texas-Mexico border
stories.summary: Discovery was made by Union Pacific employee inspecting stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey north Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Rail workers in Texas found six people dead inside a boxcar at a yard close to the Mexican border on Sunday afternoon, officials said. The discovery was made by a Union Pacific employee inspecting the stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey north, a spokesperson for the Laredo police departm

stories.title: Trump nominates new US ambassadors to Slovakia, Jamaica
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has nominated Kari Lake and Doug Mastriano — two allies who waged failed bids for governor in battleground states — to diplomatic posts. The White House announced Monday that Trump has nominated Lake to be ambassador to Jamaica and Mastriano to be ambassador to Slovakia. Both nominations require Senate confirmation. Mastriano, who ran for governor of Pennsylvania, and Lake, who lost in Arizona, both embraced the president and his baseless election conspiracies and were rej

stories.title: California gubernatorial candidate Steve Hilton mocked for misidentifying a ‘street taco’
stories.summary: The British political strategist drew swift criticism in a state where tacos are practically the official dish Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Polls have shown California voters have been largely disengaged from the upcoming election for governor, but over the weekend one candidate managed to capture the public’s attention – and ire. Steve Hilton, the British political strategist seeking the state’s top office, drew derision after posting a video outside a southern California l

stories.title: Poland says it expects US to extradite ex-minister who fled from Hungary
stories.summary: Trump reportedly involved in securing visa for Zbigniew Ziobro, who is wanted in Warsaw on criminal charges Poland has said it expects Washington to extradite a former justice minister wanted on criminal charges after reports emerged that he had fled to the US from Hungary, where the former prime minister Viktor Orbán had granted him asylum. “You can’t hide these days. You can flee, you can delay it for a while, but eventually your options run out,” Poland’s foreign minister, Radosław Sikorski,

stories.title: EU greenlights sanctions on Israeli West Bank settlers
stories.summary: Long blocked by Hungary's former government, the punitive EU measures are aimed at "extremist" Israeli settlers in the occupied West Bank. Israel has denounced the move as "arbitrary and political."

stories.title: EU approves sanctions on Israeli settlers after Hungarian backing
stories.summary: The sanctions — which include asset freezes and travel bans and will also target Hamas members — come amid growing international criticism of Israel’s settlement expansion and escalating violence in the occupied territories.

stories.title: Állami százmilliók vándoroltak Szijjártó rokonainak cégéhez, amely finn versenypályát vett
stories.summary: Legalább 600 millió forint állami megbízás és támogatás segítette a volt külügyminiszter testvérének és sógorának vállalatát a miniszteri megbízatásának kezdete óta.

stories.title: Roszatom-vezér: Az új magyar kormány összes kérdését meg fogjuk válaszolni Paks II.-ről
stories.summary: Alekszej Lihacsov szerint mindkét fél célja, hogy ez a fontos projekt a lehető leggyorsabban, leghatékonyabban és a legjobb áron valósuljon meg.

stories.title: Három csapattal is megnyerte a Bajnokok Ligáját, aztán stadiont építtetett a szülővárosának
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. BL-sztorik, hatodik rész.

stories.title: Tényleg egészségesebb lesz a krumpli, a tészta és a rizs, ha másnap eszem meg?
stories.summary: Ha megfőzzük, de a hűtőbe tesszük, és csak másnap eszünk belőle, kevésbé fogja megemelni a vércukorszintet, és egy csomó más szempontból is egészségesebb lesz. De mi az a rezisztens keményítő?

stories.title: Gajdos László a szombathelyi azbesztszennyezés helyszínére látogat
stories.summary: A leendő miniszter szerint Magyar Péter számára is prioritás a helyzet megoldása.

stories.title: A Magyar Reklámszövetség örül annak, hogy a Tisza-kormány továbbra is 0 százalékon tartja a reklámadót
stories.summary: A szervezet szerint a reklámadó a teljes magyar gazdaságra nézve is káros lenne.

stories.title: Rogán Antal és Pintér Sándor minisztériumaiban folytatta a tárlatvezetést Magyar Péter, fotókat is posztolt
stories.summary: Az új kormányfő szivarszobáról és körpanorámás luxusteraszról mutatott képeket, és videós beszámolót is ígért.

stories.title: Német László bíboros: Évek alatt a világegyháztól messze, de a politikához közel került a magyar egyház
stories.summary: Német László politikai alapon működő egyházról, elhallgattatásról és pénzelvonásról is írt. Arra szólította fel az egyházat, hogy a megújulás és az önkritika felé forduljon.

stories.title: Lőrincz Viktória: Budapestet nem lehet fejlesztési ellenfélként kezelni, a vidék ezután nem mellékes ügy lesz
stories.summary: A leendő vidék- és településfejlesztési miniszter arról beszélt a bizottsági meghallgatásán, hogy Budapest-törvényre van szükség, erős vidék nélkül pedig nincs fenntartható város. Az összes egyéni választókerületben ki fogják vizsgálni, hogy korábban mire mentek fejlesztési pénzek, mert ezekről sokszor politikai alapon döntöttek.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán: Műemlékeket bontottak le, mert jakuzzit kellett építeni a helyükre
stories.summary: A kulturális miniszterjelölt a keddi meghallgatásán kemény vádbeszédet mondott az előző kormányzással szemben a Művelődési Bizottságban.

stories.title: Bemutatták a Szociális és Családügyi Minisztérium három új államtitkárát
stories.summary: Gyurkó Szilvia mellett Barna-Szabó Tímea tiszás képviselő is Kátai-Németh Vilmos mellett fog dolgozni, a leendő miniszter pénteken mutatja be a többi államtitkárát.

stories.title: Videón mutatja be Magyar Péter Rogán minisztériumát: Ha valaki ezeket az épületeket látja, akkor azt hiszi, hogy Svájcban élünk
stories.summary: Az új miniszterelnök a Karmelita után a Miniszterelnöki Kabinetiroda és a Belügyminisztérium épületeit is bemutatta belülről.

stories.title: Krasznahorkai László kapta idén az Artisjus Irodalmi Nagydíjat
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas író A magyar nemzet biztonsága című regényéért és teljes életművéért részesült az elismerésben.

stories.title: Rosatom chief: We will answer all questions the new Hungarian government may have about Paks II
stories.summary: According to Alexey Likhachev, both parties are interested in completing the project as quickly, efficiently, and cost-effectively as possible.

stories.title: Tenerifén fogtak el egy Magyarországról elmenekült szexuális bűnözőt
stories.summary: Kiskorúak ellen követett el bűncselekményeket.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán: A magyar ember most újra úgy ismert a világban, mint egy szabadságszerető nép
stories.summary: A leendő miniszter arról beszélt, hogy az Orbán-kormány teljesen elszakadt az emberektől, a párbeszéd teljesen megszűnt. Tarr szomorú és elnyomott országról beszélt, aminek a helyzete most siralmas.

stories.title: A Budapesti Metropolitan Egyetem rektora is lemondott az ÁSZ vizsgálata és feljelentése után
stories.summary: Múlt héten derült ki, hogy a METU elnök-vezérigazgatója, Matolcsy György korábbi kabinetfőnöke is távozik az egyetem éléről.

stories.title: Magyar Péter levélben szólította fel a leköszönő minisztereket, adjanak azonnali tájékoztatást a szombat óta vállalt kötelezettségekről
stories.summary: Hétfő 22 órát szabta meg határidőnek.

stories.title: A GVH és a Legfőbb Ügyészség is reagált Magyar lemondási felszólítására
stories.summary: Megkerestük a lemondásra felszólított közjogi méltóságokat, a GVH válaszában többek között azt írta, elnöküket eskü kötelezi a hivatali szolgálatának ellátására.

stories.title: Magyar Péter élőben tartott tárlatvezetést a Karmelitában
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök mások mellett Vitézy Dáviddal és Orbán Anitával járta körbe a Karmelita épületét, olvasott a Bibliából és benézett Orbán Viktor és Semjén Zsolt korábbi irodájába is.

stories.title: Hungary’s next health minister Zsolt Hegedűs dances at Magyar celebration
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered outside the Hungarian parliament building to celebrate the official inauguration of Peter Magyar as prime minister. Attendees included incoming Health Minister Zsolt Hegedus, who went viral for his dancing on the night of the country's April election.

stories.title: Trump open to shifting US troops from Germany to Poland
stories.summary: Will US troops withdrawn from Germany be moved to Poland? When asked, US President Donald Trump said Warsaw favours the idea, and it is feasible.

stories.title: "We've lived with them for 80 years": Inside a German town that US troops could soon leave after Donald Trump's withdrawal threat
stories.summary: If US President Donald Trump is serious, 5,000 US soldiers could be withdrawn from Germany. This would include the Stryker Brigade in Vilseck. The town is in shock.

stories.title: Hungary: New govt intent on initiating swift systemic change
stories.summary: Peter Magyar and his government are determined to push ahead with change at full throttle. But even before the cabinet is sworn in, there has been a "resignation." And questions remain about Magyar's Ukraine policy.

stories.title: Three-day ceasefire ends with fresh wave of Russian attacks on Ukraine – Europe live
stories.summary: At least one person killed as Moscow launches drone strikes on energy facilities and apartments Today’s talks in Brussels come as the three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine announced by president Donald Trump last week ended with a fresh wave of Russian strikes. AFP reported that Moscow launched a wave of more than 200 attack drones that damaged energy facilities and apartment buildings, killing at least one person. “Attack drones were shot down in the Dnipro, Zhytomyr, Mykolaiv, Sumy, K

stories.title: Merz flubs his comeback tour
stories.summary: BERLIN — As Friedrich Merz’s popularity plunges to new lows — compounding his already-weak coalition’s troubles little more than a year into office — the chancellor is traveling the country to persuade Germans that things remain on the right track. It’s not going well. Instead, Merz looks like he is only compounding his problems, often appearing out of touch and further alienating some of the very voters he can least afford to lose. A stop in Salzwedel, a town lined with half-timbered homes

stories.title: New EU medicines law aims to bypass India, China on key drugs
stories.summary: The EU will look to boost local production of medicines through state aid and rewards for “made in EU” products, after legislators clinched a deal early Tuesday morning. After more than 12 hours of talks, the European Parliament and the Council of the EU agreed to introduce public procurement criteria that move away from favoring the cheapest medicines and instead prioritize their security of supply. Europe’s new law seeks to reduce its dependencies on countries including India

stories.title: Mass starvation looms if fertilisers can’t pass key waterway, UN warns – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. See full report: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire frays Turkey’s foreign minister, Hakan Fidan, will visit Qatar later today for talks on the war, its impact on the region and efforts to ensure navigational safety in the strait of Hormuz is resumed, a Turkish diplomatic source told the Reuters news agency. Turkey, which neighbours Iran, has been in close contact with the US, Iran and mediator Pakistan since the start of the co

stories.title: Virginia Democrats ask conservative-majority US supreme court to restore congressional map approved by voters – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Sign up for the Breaking News US email Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Trump reiterated that Iran’s peace proposal was “just unacceptable”. The president went on to insist that he had a “very simple plan”, and maintained that Tehran could not have a nuclear weapon, without elaborating on the next negotiating steps. Continue reading...

stories.title: Chaos in Philippines as Duterte ally wanted by ICC takes refuge in senate to avoid arrest
stories.summary: Former police chief Ronald dela Rosa spends night at senate office after another Duterte ally offers protective custody The unusual pursuit was captured on CCTV cameras inside the Philippine senate. Ronald dela Rosa, a longtime ally of the former president Rodrigo Duterte, raced along the hallways of the upper house complex, stumbling on the staircase, as he fled government agents. “They want to forcibly bring me to The Hague, to surrender me there,” Dela Rosa said later on a Facebook livestream

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia breaks ceasefire overnight, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy has said that Moscow launched some 200 drone strikes in a single night, bringing an end to a temporary truce. Moscow meanwhile said it intercepted 27 Ukrainian drones overnight.

stories.title: EU social media ban could come this summer, von der Leyen says
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen has said that raising the social media age limit across the European Union is on the table, and a proposal could come as soon as this summer. “I believe we must consider a social media delay,” von der Leyen said at a conference in Copenhagen on how to protect children in the age of artificial intelligence. “Depending on the results, we could come up with a legal proposal this summer.” Von der Leyen said she didn’t w

stories.title: The big questions hanging over the Trump-Xi meeting in China
stories.summary: Taiwan, tariffs and the strait of Hormuz are on the meeting’s agenda for Beijing – but will the US president be forced to ask for help in ending his war with Iran? On 20 February, a White House official confirmed that US president Donald Trump would be travelling to Beijing the following month to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping. Top of the agenda: the US-China trade war. One week later, Trump approved joint strikes with Israel against Iran, starting a new war in the Middle East. Its ramifica

stories.title: Trump officials cancel rule that made conservation a ‘use’ of public lands
stories.summary: Move comes as administration seeks to boost drilling, logging, mining and grazing on taxpayer-owned land The interior department is canceling a rule that put conservation on equal footing with development, as Donald Trump’s administration eases restrictions on industries and seeks to boost drilling, logging, mining and grazing on taxpayer-owned land. The 2024 rule adopted under former president Joe Biden was meant to refocus the interior department’s Bureau of Land Management, which oversees abo

stories.title: Zelenskyy’s ex-chief of staff accused in Ukraine corruption investigation
stories.summary: Anti-graft agencies say Andriy Yermak suspected of participating in criminal group that laundered $10.5m in housing project, which he denies ownership in Ukrainian authorities have named Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s powerful former chief of staff as a suspect in a major corruption investigation, a move likely to pile pressure on the president’s office at a sensitive moment in the war with Russia. Kyiv’s political class was rocked by a wide-ranging probe last year that had fuelled public anger and promp

stories.title: Five former Carillion executives banned by accountancy regulator
stories.summary: Richard Adam, Zafar Khan and three others are also fined by Financial Reporting Council Business live – latest updates Five former executives at the collapsed government contractor Carillion have been banned by the UK’s accountancy regulator, effectively ending the career of its former finance chief, after they “acted recklessly”. Before Carillion collapsed into compulsory liquidation in January 2018 – one of the biggest corporate failures in UK history – it was a large multinational constructio

stories.title: Fortescue ordered to pay Yindjibarndi traditional owners $150m in record native title payout
stories.summary: Federal court found miner’s Solomon Hub iron ore mine caused cultural and social harm, including the destruction of 140 heritage sites Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Mining company Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150m in compensation to traditional owners over cultural losses caused by the multibillion-dollar Solomon Hub iron ore mine – the largest compensation payout in native title history. The mine, which

stories.title: Labor dropped their long-awaited gambling report on budget day. Were they betting no one would notice?
stories.summary: The government’s response to the Murphy report is hardly dynamite. Harm advocates, gambling operators and advertisers are all waiting to see whether Labor’s rhetoric will be matched by action The Labor government decided to release its contentious, much-delayed response to Peta Murphy’s report on gambling on one of the biggest political news days of the year. They dropped it while the nation’s federal political journalists were trapped in budget lockup. Continue reading...

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran could enrich uranium to weapons grade if attacked, senior lawmaker warns
stories.summary: Ebrahim Rezaei says in social media post that the country could enrich its stocks of uranium to 90% and will review such a move in parliament Israel sent Iron Dome anti-missile batteries and personnel to operate them to the UAE to defend the country during the war with Iran, the US ambassador to Israel, Mike Huckabee, said at an event in Tel Aviv, Israel. “I’d like to say a word of appreciation for United Arab Emirates, the first Abraham accord member,” Huckabee said at the Tel Aviv conference.

stories.title: Federal budget 2026 live updates: Australia government budget announcement and speech tonight – latest news
stories.summary: Treasurer expected to announce changes to the capital gains tax discount when he delivers the 2026 federal budget later tonight. You don’t make more of something by increasing ta

stories.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran on ‘life support’ after rejecting peace proposals
stories.summary: US president says he is considering restarting naval escorts in strait of Hormuz in attempt to end Iranian blockade Donald Trump has said the ceasefire with Iran is on “life support” and that he is considering restarting US navy military escorts of ships through the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to end the Iranian blockade of the vital waterway. The US president dismissed Iran’s peace proposals as stupid, and denied he was under any domestic pressure to reach a deal. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran war: Trump says ceasefire 'on life support'
stories.summary: After rejecting Tehran's response to the US peace proposal, President Donald Trump warned that the ceasefire in the war with Iran was "on life support." Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Ukraine: EU sanctions Russians over 'systematic unlawful deportation' of children
stories.summary: The EU is sanctioning 16 individuals and seven entities over alleged child abductions. The UK also issued fresh sanctions, and Germany's defense minister visited Kyiv amid a shaky ceasefire. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
stories.summary: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

stories.title: Ex-Philippine leader Duterte's drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest
stories.summary: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, a former national police chief, has sought refuge in the senate.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire 'on life support,' Trump says as deadlock in negotiations deepens
stories.summary: The stalled diplomacy and recent exchanges of fire could tip the Middle East back into open warfare and prolong the worldwide energy crisis sparked by the conflict.

stories.title: Russia launches strikes at Ukraine, hours after expiration of 72-hour truce
stories.summary: On Sunday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine would retaliate if Russia decided "to return to a full-scale war."

stories.title: Pakistan struck a rehab centre and killed 269 Afghans. Their families want to know why
stories.summary: There are calls to investigate the 16 March airstrike - which the UN says likely killed more - as a war crime.

stories.title: Starmer in crisis as top Cabinet minister pushes for exit plan — live updates
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s premiership is in grave peril after a key member of his Cabinet told him to set out an exit plan following dire nationwide election results — and a minister resigned. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood last night told Starmer to oversee an orderly transition of power and consider setting out a timeline for his departure. The PM hosts a weekly Cabinet meeting at 9.30 a.m. More than 70 Labour MPs have now called on Starmer to go. Energy Minister Miatta Fahnbulleh —

stories.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire frays
stories.summary: US president expresses ire at Tehran’s reported demands, as drones strike Gulf nations and Israel warns war ‘not over’ Donald Trump has rejected an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable”, on a day the month-old ceasefire showed signs of fraying as drone strikes were reported around the region and Benjamin Netanyahu warned the war was “not over”. The Iranian counter-proposal was passed to Washington through Pakistani mediators. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mayor of California city resigns over charges of being a foreign agent of China
stories.summary: Eileen Wang, 58, mayor of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty over the felony count brought by the justice department Eileen Wang, the mayor of a southern California city, resigned suddenly on Monday after the US Department of Justice (DoJ) announced she had been charged with acting as an illegal foreign agent of China. Wang, 58, agreed to plead guilty to the felony count and could face a sentence of 10 years in prison. Continue reading...

stories.title: Yes, Europe’s leaders are finally standing up to Trump. Here’s why | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz’s criticism of the US president was not a solo run. It was born of the realisation that US leverage has slipped Friedrich Merz’s criticism of Donald Trump last month reflected more than a moment of personal candour or a split between Berlin and the White House. It pointed to a broader shift under way among European leaders. Increasingly they are willing to publicly confront the Trump administration on issues ranging from Iran to Ukraine and European sovereignty. The Trump administ

stories.title: ‘I couldn’t breathe’: the sinister spread of France’s killer seaweed
stories.summary: After a series of deaths on the beaches of Brittany, one bereaved family set out to prove the foul-smelling bloom was to blame When her phone rang at around 5pm on 8 September 2016, Rosy Auffray was still at work. It was one of her daughters, distressed, calling to tell her that their father, Jean-René, had not come back from his daily run. Only the family dog had returned, alone and exhausted. Rosy rushed back home. When she arrived, Rosy noticed that the dog was behaving bizarrely: she refused

stories.title: Cannes spotlight reverts to auteurs as Hollywood retreats from film festival
stories.summary: Absence of big US films heralds renewed focus on international cinema that underpins festival’s reputation For decades, Cannes has occupied a unique place in the cultural imagination – not just as the world’s most prestigious film festival, but as Hollywood’s most glamorous overseas outpost. From Grace Kelly on the Croisette, Quentin Tarantino and Uma Thurman at the Pulp Fiction premiere, Julia Roberts walking barefoot up the red carpet, to Tom Cruise shutting down the Riviera with fighter jets

stories.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
stories.summary: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in ink Japan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade. Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue r

stories.title: It’s parliament, not Love Island: Queensland’s ministerial affair drama becomes a sordid distraction
stories.summary: Tuesday’s question time seemed more suited to prime time; full of sordid allegations, only the cast seems far less likable Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Question time in the Queensland parliament is not a ratings juggernaut. Nielsen doesn’t publish ratings data about the live stream from Brisbane’s George Street, but one imagines the numbers of voters tuning in to watch our state politicians flagellate on a wee

stories.title: Mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby asks politicians not to leverage child’s death: ‘My heart is broken into a million pieces’
stories.summary: Labor introduced condolence motions in parliament on Tuesday to honour the five-year-old who was allegedly murdered in Alice Springs last month Warning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby has directly asked politicians not to use the death of her daughter for “reasons that do not honour and respect my baby gi

stories.title: Trump heads to China to spread the gospel of American tech while emulating Xi Jinping on AI
stories.summary: Tim Cook and Elon Musk, among other tech CEOS, will accompany the US president on a trip to China Donald Trump is heading to China this week. If his guest list is any clue, he wants to discuss technology with Xi Jinping , though perhaps after the war in Iran. On Monday, news broke that outgoing Apple CEO, Tim Cook, as well as SpaceX and Tesla CEO, Elon Musk, would join the US president. Other guests from the tech sphere include Meta’s recently appointed president, Dina Powell McCormick; Sanjay M

stories.title: Gaborone gold rush: how Botswana rose to the top of men’s sprinting
stories.summary: Country with a population of just 2.5m credits investment in young athletes for its rise but this progress is under threat It was a fairytale ending to the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone. In the final strait, Collen Kebinatshipi surged past South Africa’s Zakithi Nene to win the men’s 4x400m relay for Botswana. The home crowd, a sea of light blue, went wild. “It means so many things to us,” Letsile Tebogo, 22, the reigning 200m Olympic champion, who ran the second leg, told reporters afterwa

stories.title: Labour must offer more than ‘better managed decline’ on economy, MPs urge
stories.summary: Soft-left Tribune group issues thinly disguised attack on Keir Starmer, calling for bold policy renewal Business live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates An influential group of MPs has said that Labour needs an urgent renewal of economic strategy to offer voters “more than better management of decline” before the next general election. With Keir Starmer fighting to ward off a leadership challenge , the leading backbenchers from the soft-left Tribune group published a series of es

stories.title: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict at record high in 2025
stories.summary: The 32.3m surpasses those caused by disasters for the first time, as 82.2m people displaced in total around world The number of internal displacements triggered by conflict or violence around the world reached a record high in 2025, surpassing the number of disaster-driven internal displacements for the first time. A report published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC) shows that by the end of 2025 there were 32.3m conflict-driven internal displacements. That is 60% higher than

stories.title: Minister resigns from Starmer government with call for PM to quit
stories.summary: Communities minister Miatta Fahnbulleh is first minister to leave government after heavy election losses UK politics live – latest updates Miatta Fahnbulleh has become the first minister to resign from Keir Starmer’s government, calling on the prime minister to quit. The communities minister’s resignation came as one of Starmer’s closest aides declined to say whether he would lead Labour into the next election amid mounting calls for him to resign. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK borrowing costs hit highest since 1998 amid Starmer uncertainty
stories.summary: Bond yields soar and pound falls against dollar as investors brace for potential Labour leadership change Business live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Long-term UK borrowing costs have soared to the highest level in nearly three decades while the pound and stocks fell, as investors braced for a potential change of leadership, with cabinet ministers urging Keir Starmer to quit . Starmer is consulting colleagues before a crunch cabinet meeting on Tuesday morning that comes afte

stories.title: First minister resigns and calls for Starmer’s resignation ahead of critical cabinet meeting – UK politics live
stories.summary: Miatta Fahnbulleh – minister for devolution, faith and communities – has become the first minister to go Starmer on the brink as cabinet ministers urge him to quit Here are some pictures from No 10 this morning. Darren Jones , the chief secretary to the PM, is now being interviewed on the Today programme. Nick Robinson , the presenter, is asking him if he knows whether Keir Starmer has decided how to respond to the pressure on him to resign. Jones is avoiding the question, as he did on Sky News

stories.title: ‘Truly terrifying’: Alberta voter data breach raises fears for Canada’s electoral integrity
stories.summary: Debates over secession overshadowed by revelations separatist-linked group gained access to list of electors The illegal use of voter information by rightwing separatists in the province of Alberta has raised fresh fears over Canada’s electoral integrity by making valuable and “incredibly confidential” personal data easily accessible to malicious actors, security experts have warned. The data breach, one of the largest in Canadian history, has prompted warnings of a “truly terrifying” new battle

stories.title: Chicago teen who pushed for parents’ release from ICE custody dies of cancer
stories.summary: Kevin González, 18-year-old who had terminal colon cancer, died shortly after reuniting with his parents in Mexico A Chicago-born teen who advocated for his parents’ release from US immigration authorities’ custody while fighting terminal cancer has died shortly after reuniting with them in Mexico, his family has told media outlets. The parents of 18-year-old Kevin González had been taken into Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) custody in Arizona in mid-April after they crossed the US b

stories.title: Ukrainian children held in Russia: militarized, 'reeducated'
stories.summary: A Ukrainian initiative to repatriate abducted Ukrainian children reports that at least 20,000 girls and boys are being held by Russian families and authorities. Can these children ever find their way home?

stories.title: South Africa's Ramaphosa refuses to resign over cash scandal
stories.summary: Despite the establishment of an impeachment committee, South African President Cyril Ramaphosa has ruled out resigning over the cash heist scandal.

stories.title: US: LA area mayor to plead guilty to acting as Chinese agent
stories.summary: The mayor of a Los Angeles suburb with a heavy Chinese-American presence has resigned after admitting to acting as a Chinese propaganda agent. Eileen Wang now faces a maximum penalty of 10 years in jail.

stories.title: EU negotiators agree new rules on vital medicines
stories.summary: A new agreement aims to tackle the drug shortages that have plagued the bloc. Among other things, it would make it easier to use public funds to support the production of essential medicines.

stories.title: What now for the role of high representative in Bosnia?
stories.summary: Christian Schmidt's departure as high representative in Bosnia and Herzegovina doesn't look like a routine diplomatic handover. It seems to indicate that international powers want something different from the role.

stories.title: India: New Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay cracks down on state-run liquor shops
stories.summary: In his first major decision since becoming the chief minister of Tamil Nadu, Vijay has ordered 717 state-run liquor stores near religious places and schools to shut down.

stories.title: Young hijab-wearing German beauty contestant takes on the AfD
stories.summary: As a Miss Germany contestant, Büsra Sayed wore a hijab on stage. This had never happened before — and the AfD didn't like it. Her response went viral.

stories.title: Germany news: 1-in-6 retailers fear for future
stories.summary: About one-in-12 German businesses worry about survival, with twice as many retailers jittery. Meanwhile in Stuttgart, five pro-Palestinian activists face trial over attacking an Israeli defense company site. Follow DW.

stories.title: Watch: Alaska town sees its last sunset until August
stories.summary: Residents of Utqiagvik, the United States' northernmost city, will experience 84 days of midnight sun and uninterrupted daylight.

stories.title: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call
stories.summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he would not resign on Monday evening after days of speculation.

stories.title: I led hikers up an Indonesian volcano - and then it erupted
stories.summary: An Indonesian guide who led hikers up Mount Dukono has described the moment of Friday's eruption which killed three people.

stories.title: Why welfare isn't winning elections in India like it used to
stories.summary: India's welfare politics still matters, but voters increasingly want jobs, growth and dignity too.

stories.title: How the Trump-Xi summit could set superpower relations for many years to come
stories.summary: Trump's whirlwind visit to China is the most significant for years - but will it bear fruit?

stories.title: US passengers quarantining in Nebraska as officials say public hantavirus risk 'very low'
stories.summary: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, 16 in Nebraska and another six passengers in Canada.

stories.title: US in closely guarded talks to open new bases in Greenland
stories.summary: It is seeking to open three bases in the south of the Arctic territory, according to multiple officials familiar with the talks.

stories.title: Israel passes law to allow death penalty and public trials for those linked to 7 October
stories.summary: Israel passes a law that permits the death penalty and public trials for those involved in the unprecedented Hamas-led attacks.

stories.title: Last passengers leave virus-hit cruise ship as three more test positive
stories.summary: An American and a French national who have returned home have tested positive for hantavirus.

stories.title: France seeks to move beyond colonial ties by meeting African leaders in Kenya
stories.summary: In a first since these meetings started in the 1970s, this move reflects a change in France's thinking.

stories.title: German ultrarunner Arda Saatçi completes 600-kilometre Death Valley to Los Angeles challenge
stories.summary: German athlete Arda Saatçi completed a 600-kilometre ultramarathon challenge from Death Valley to Los Angeles after running for more than 123 hours.

stories.title: Elon Musk and Tim Cook among CEOs expected to accompany Trump on China trip
stories.summary: A total of 17 US executives are set to join the president on his visit, where he will meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

stories.title: Final passengers disembark hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
stories.summary: The final passengers aboard the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius disembarked in Tenerife before boarding flights to quarantine facilities in more than 20 countries.

stories.title: China should stop hoarding food and fertiliser, says former World Bank chief
stories.summary: David Malpass also said that Beijing's claim to be a developing nation was no longer credible.

stories.title: Philippine VP Sara Duterte impeached for a second time
stories.summary: If convicted in a Senate trial, which is uncertain, she will be disqualified from running for president.

stories.title: French farmers dump manure at TotalEnergies refinery in fuel price protest
stories.summary: French farmers have staged a protest outside a major oil refinery near Lyon, dumping manure and rubbish at the entrance in anger over rising fuel costs and falling incomes.

stories.title: Sudan drone strikes killed at least 880 civilians between January and April, UN says
stories.summary: Most of the civilian deaths attributed to drone strikes in the first three months of the year were recorded in the Kordofan region and Darfur, although the strikes were also increasingly spreading beyond both regions.

stories.title: Netflix sued by Texas AG for alleged surveillance, addictive features
stories.summary: Texas sued Netflix on Monday for allegedly tracking, collecting and profiting from data produced by children and other customers without their knowledge. Attorney General Ken Paxton  sued the streaming giant  under Texas laws against deceptive business practices, alleging that Netflix billed itself as a “safe respite” from the surveillance and advertising that characterize other big tech platforms while engaging in similar data harvesting practices. The suit also claims that Netflix de

stories.title: Sharp drop in ‘forever chemicals’ in seabird eggs hailed as win for regulation
stories.summary: Levels of Pfas in northern gannet eggs in Canada fell up to 74% over 55-year period of study Levels of some of the most dangerous Pfas compounds have dramatically fallen in Canadian seabird eggs, which the authors of a new peer-reviewed study say illustrates how regulations are effective. Researchers looked at Pfas levels in the eggs of northern gannets in the St Lawrence Seaway basin over a 55-year period. Pfas levels shot up from the 1960s through the peak of the chemicals’ use in the late 199

stories.title: Philippine presidential hopeful Sara Duterte impeached for second time
stories.summary: Vice-president is accused of misusing public funds and threatening the lives of President Marcos Jr and his wife The Philippine vice-president, Sara Duterte, has been impeached over allegations she misused public funds, amassed unexplained wealth and threatened the lives of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr and his wife, in a case that could complicate her presidential ambitions. Duterte, the daughter of the detained former president Rodrigo Duterte, was impeached by an overwhelming majority of lawm

stories.title: Evacuated US and French MV Hondius passengers test positive for hantavirus
stories.summary: French woman taken to Paris in serious condition while American flown to Nebraska is asymptomatic, say officials Europe live – latest updates A French woman and an American national evacuated from the cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive for the virus, as the complex operation to repatriate those onboard continued on Monday. The French woman was one of five French passengers who disembarked from the ship in Tenerife on Sunday before being flown to a hosp

stories.title: Israeli troops jailed for desecration of Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
stories.summary: IDF says two soldiers will spend weeks in military prison over incident it said was viewed with great severity Two Israeli soldiers will spend weeks in military prison for the desecration of a Christian object after one stuck a cigarette in the mouth of a statue of the Virgin Mary in southern Lebanon and the other photographed it. The photo of the soldier, a cigarette dangling from his own mouth, went viral and sparked widespread outrage. It was the latest act by Israeli forces in southern Leban

stories.title: Putin is in a ‘weaker position than ever before’, says EU’s Kallas – as it happened
stories.summary: The bloc’s foreign policy chief said recent remarks from the Russian leader suggested the war may be coming to an end Russian affairs reporter The EU on Monday dismissed Vladimir Putin’s suggestion that the Kremlin-friendly former German chancellor Gerhard Schröder could serve as a European mediator in peace talks aimed at ending the war in Ukraine. Continue reading...

stories.title: Drug gang attacks ‘force hundreds of Indigenous families to flee’ in Mexico
stories.summary: Violence in Guerrero state has driven as many as 1,000 households from their homes, rights group says Hundreds of Indigenous families have been forced to flee their homes in the mountains of central Mexico by intense attacks from a local criminal group, including drone bombings, an Indigenous rights organisation said on Monday . A gang known as Los Ardillos has been carrying out attacks in Guerrero state for years, but they started to intensify last week. Villages were subjected to eight hours o

stories.title: Somali piracy adds new strain to global shipping and trade routes
stories.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

stories.title: Endemic, epidemic, pandemic: What's the difference?
stories.summary: The Andes hantavirus outbreak shows how easily disease labels are misunderstood — and why they describe patterns of spread, not how dangerous a virus is.

stories.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer 'not walking away' after local election mauling
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has said he takes "responsibility" for Labour's poor election performance, but also for putting matters right again. "I know I have my doubters and I know I need to prove them wrong, and I will," he said.

stories.title: What will happen to Iran's nuclear material?
stories.summary: Iran is believed to have over 440 kilograms of enriched uranium, which could be turned into weapons-grade nuclear material. Despite Trump's pledge to get the "nuclear dust," Tehran has kept it out of US reach.

stories.title: Nigeria's solar boom faces cost and policy barriers
stories.summary: Nigeria's rising solar imports signal a search for alternatives to unreliable grid power and high fuel costs. But adoption is being held back by steep upfront prices, limited financing and policy uncertainty.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How it differs from COVID
stories.summary: In 2018-2019, the Andes virus hantavirus spread person-to-person in Argentina. Eleven people died — but it was stopped. Analysis of that outbreak shows the current one can also be stopped.

stories.title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
stories.summary: Famous for designing the Jewish Museum Berlin and the Ground Zero project in New York, the architect has created dozens of memorable buildings worldwide.

stories.title: Religion steers Bengal politics in Bangladesh, India — again
stories.summary: Bengal, a region known for its secular traditions, is seeing a rise of religion in politics as Islamists grow stronger on the Bangladesh side of the border and Hindu nationalist BJP takes power in India's West Bengal.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained as passengers return?
stories.summary: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship are returning to their home countries, some with symptoms, many without. What happens to these people and what is being done to contain the spread?

stories.title: White House press dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty
stories.summary: A California man has been charged with attempting to assassinate Trump at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

stories.title: Slovenia, Spain and Ireland won't air Eurovision over Israel's participation
stories.summary: The three European countries are boycotting the popular song contest over Israel's participation in it. More countries have said they won't be taking part.

stories.title: Hundreds of ships mark Hamburg harbour’s 837th anniversary
stories.summary: Hamburg celebrated the 837th anniversary of its harbour with a maritime parade featuring around 250 ships, from historic sailing vessels to modern coast guard boats.

stories.title: Turkish Airlines plane evacuated after landing gear fire in Nepal, officials say
stories.summary: The Himalayan nation is home to some of the world's most remote and tricky runways, flanked by snow-capped peaks and terrain that poses a challenge even for accomplished pilots.

stories.title: Trump's China visit set to test fragile truce
stories.summary: The first US presidential visit to China in almost 10 years will test a fragile tariff truce.

stories.title: Iran warns Europe against sending warships to Hormuz as US talks gap widens
stories.summary: Tehran has warned Europe against the idea of sending warships to the key waterway, as it outlined a conditional proposal on uranium, sanctions relief and a Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.title: Police find body believed to be of fugitive Australian shooter
stories.summary: A body found in New South Wales is thought to be that of a man wanted over a triple murder.

stories.title: Ailing Iranian Nobel laureate given bail and hospital transfer
stories.summary: Iranian authorities granted human rights activist Narges Mohammadi bail amid growing concern over her poor health in prison.

stories.title: Watch: Humanoid robot becomes Buddhist monk in South Korea
stories.summary: A humanoid robot named Gabi became an honorary Buddhist monk during an ordination ceremony held at Seoul’s Jogye Temple on May 6, ahead of Buddha’s birthday celebrations in South Korea.

stories.title: Watch: Soldier on parachuting to remote island to bring Hantavirus aid
stories.summary: A specialist team helped supply critical medical support to Tristan da Cunha in the South Atlantic.

stories.title: Bosnia's powerful peace envoy quits, with questions over role's future
stories.summary: Christian Schmidt had been in the role since 2021, and departs after appearing to lose the backing of the United States.

stories.title: 'I will make an order': Macron asks audience to leave if they want to chat during Nairobi summit
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron interrupted a speaker at the Africa Forward summit in Nairobi to call for silence from a noisy audience, telling attendees to leave the room if they wanted to continue chatting during the speeches.

stories.title: Whales could be harmed by diverted ships avoiding Middle East, scientists warn
stories.summary: Ships have rerouted around the Cape of Good Hope since 2023 to avoid conflicts in the Middle East.

stories.title: Man charged with attempting to kill US President Donald Trump pleads not guilty
stories.summary: Besides the attempted-assassination count, Cole Tomas Allen is also charged with assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two additional firearms counts.

stories.title: Washington dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty
stories.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempting to assassinate US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: 'Total lack of respect': Macron interrupts speaker to ask for silence
stories.summary: The French president stood up during a conference in Kenya to tell the audience to quieten down, saying it was “impossible” for speakers to be heard.

stories.title: Dozens of Nigerian fishermen feared dead after Chad air strikes on Boko Haram
stories.summary: A fishermen's leader tells the BBC that 40 people are feared dead - either in the strikes or drowning as they tried to flee.

stories.title: Pashinyan says Karabakh 'was not ours' as Armenia and Azerbaijan look to future
stories.summary: In an extraordinary admission, Armenia’s PM Nikol Pashinyan declared that Karabakh was not Armenia’s territory and that the Armenian movement for it was “a fatal mistake,” as Yerevan forges its peace with Azerbaijan and its decisive pro-EU course.

stories.title: Oil price predicted to remain above $100 for rest of year
stories.summary: Investment bank JP Morgan expects oil to remain in the "low $100s" even if the Strait of Hormuz were to reopen next month.

stories.title: International Feline Contest: Europe’s 'most beautiful cats' gather in Sofia
stories.summary: Cat lovers from across Europe gathered in Sofia, Bulgaria, for the international Cat Show Expo, where more than 150 cats competed in a prestigious feline competition judged by experts from several countries.

stories.title: 'Britain at the heart of Europe': How Starmer's plans are going down in the EU
stories.summary: Sir Keir Starmer pledged to rebuild the UK's relationship with Europe in a defiant speech on Monday.

stories.title: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader's descendants
stories.summary: The painting is believed to have been plundered by high-ranking Nazi Hermann Goering during World War Two.

stories.title: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
stories.summary: The EU's foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences", while Israel branded the move "arbitrary".

stories.title: Bárki állt is ennek a gigantikus áfacsalásnak a hátterében, végig védve érezhette magát
stories.summary: Bár már 41 gyanúsítottja van a földgázmaffia néven ismert ügynek, az éceszgéberek még látóhatáron belül sincsenek. A gyanúsítottak között van, aki analfabéta, egy külföldiekből álló társaság pedig egykor figyelés alatt állt, mégis fel tudott szívódni.

stories.title: A Tisza miniszterei kora délután Sulyok Tamáshoz mennek, délután eskütétel, éjféltől lesz új kormány
stories.summary: Még zajlanak a miniszteri meghallgatások, délután az Országgyűlés előtt tesznek esküt az új miniszterek.

stories.title: Egyre több párttársa szólítja fel lemondásra a brit miniszterelnököt a választási vereség után
stories.summary: A munkáspárti Keir Starmer szerint a lemondásának az ország látná kárát.

stories.title: Önök láttak már átműtött óvodást? Mert én még nem láttam
stories.summary: Hétfőn meghallgatta a parlament oktatásügyi bizottsága a leendő oktatási és gyermekügyi minisztert. Lannert Judit a teljes oktatási rendszer radikális átalakítására készül.

stories.title: Kirúgtak egy vasutas szakszervezeti vezetőt, de a MÁV szerint nem azért, mert a problémákról beszélt
stories.summary: A vasút szerint a dolgozó jogellenesen hozott nyilvánosságra bizalmas információkat.

stories.title: Az ország nyugati felén is komoly esőzésre figyelmeztetnek
stories.summary: Hétfőn inkább az ország keleti része kapott a záporokból.

stories.title: Bujdosó Andrea bemutatta a frakcióvezető-helyetteseit
stories.summary: A Tisza frakcióvezetője szerint nagy feladat áll előttük.

stories.title: Lejár a kampányplakátok leszedésének határideje
stories.summary: Ha a pártok nem szedik le időben a plakátokat, kapják a számlát.

stories.title: A jogállam helyreállítását és korrupcióellenes fordulatot ígér a leendő igazságügyi miniszter
stories.summary: Görög Márta az Igazságügyi és Alkotmányügyi Bizottságának ülésén azt ígérte, hogy politkai érdekektől és ideológiáktól mentesen, kizárólag szakmai alapon kíván dolgozni. Nonprofittá alakítják a bírósági végrehajtói rendszert, csatlakozunk az Európai Ügyészséghez.

stories.title: Kátai-Németh a fideszes képviselőknek: Soha senki nem műtött át gyereket, melegshow sem volt az iskolákban
stories.summary: A leendő szociális és családügyi miniszter azt ígérte, ki fogják vizsgálni, mi történt Bicskén és a Szőlő utcában. Lesz egyenlőbánásmód- és gyermekvédelmi ombudsman, ígérte Kátai-Németh. A Tisza roma származású képviselője eközben a bizottsági ülésen azt mondta Dúró Dórának: ha zavarja, hogy egy teremben vannak, nyugodtan menjen ki.

stories.title: Közel 80 milliárd forintot szórt ki a választás előtti hónapokban a Miniszterelnöki Kabinetiroda
stories.summary: Nagyjából az összeg fele ment Balásy Gyuláék cégeinek, de 20 milliárddal a Batthyány Lajos Alapítványt, 25 millióval pedig a Parlamenti Imacsoportot is megtámogatták.

stories.title: Szájkarate: Radnaimark.hu vs. Lázár blamái
stories.summary: Keressük a kampányidőszak legbizarrabb történetét. Melyik borított ki jobban minket: a titokzatos szoba vagy a száját bármikor kinyitó Lázár János?

stories.title: Ideiglenes megállapodásra jutott az EU a gyógyszerhiány leküzdését célzó szabályozásról
stories.summary: Az elmúlt időszakban többször is volt hiány az EU-ban egyes kulcsfontosságú gyógyszerekből. Közös beszerzésekkel és új gyártói kapacitásokkal orvosolnák a problémát.

stories.title: Ruff Bálint: A valaha volt legnagyobb átvilágítást fogom elrendelni
stories.summary: A leendő kancelláriaminiszter egyből bemutatta két államtitkárjelöltjét, Csontos Bencét és Bíró-Nagy Andrást.

stories.title: Bíró-Nagy András, a Policy Solutions vezetője lesz Ruff Bálint szakpolitikai államtitkára
stories.summary: Ruff Bálint miniszterjelölt jelentette be az Igazságügyi és Alkotmányügyi Bizottság keddi meghallgatásán.

stories.title: Pert nyert a Greenpeace a Mollal szemben a gárdonyi olajszennyezés ügyében
stories.summary: Másfél évvel ezelőtt egy úszómedencényi üzemanyag folyt a földbe, jelentős része ráadásul azután, hogy a Mol észlelte a problémát. Most megsemmisítették a határozatot, ami a szervezet szerint túl enyhe kártalanítást szabott ki a vállalatra.

stories.title: Kemenesi Gábor szerint a feudális rendszert le kell vetkőzni, örök mutyi helyett verseny kell a tudományban
stories.summary: A virológus szerint véget kell vetni annak, hogy a kutatóknak jóban kell lennie a döntéseket meghozóival, mert ebből nem lesz valódi versenyképesség.

stories.title: Dolgoztam Anikó nénivel, virágokat ültettünk – hatvanpusztai munkájáról mesélt egy férfi
stories.summary: A Roma Sajtóközpontnak nyilatkozó munkás nagyjából két évig segített a volt miniszterelnök feleségének a parkrendezésben.

stories.title: Kármán András: Teljes gazdasági irányváltásra van szükség
stories.summary: A leendő pénzügyminiszter terveivel folytatódnak a miniszterjelölti meghallgatások.

stories.title: Hoppál Péter 16 év után nem indult el a pécsi Fidesz elnökválasztásán
stories.summary: A volt államtitkár Csizmadia Pétert ajánlotta maga helyett új vezetőnek, akit a közgyűlés meg is választott elnöknek.

stories.title: A Fidesz kezdeményezte az EP-ben az uniós alapértékek megsértése miatt indított eljárás megszüntetését
stories.summary: A Patrióták frakció is csatlakozott a kezdeményezéshez.

stories.title: Mészáros Lőrinc kivonulhat az eszéki focicsapat mögül
stories.summary: Állítólag már a vezetés februári leváltása is az eladást készíthette elő. A horvát sajtó szerint az ügyletben a legendás futballista, Davor Šuker is szerepet kaphat.

stories.title: Angela Rayner calls blocking Andy Burnham’s return to parliament a mistake as pressure mounts on Keir Starmer – as it happened
stories.summary: Catherine West issues ultimatum for PM as ex-minister Josh Simons joins calls for prime minister to quit At the start of her programme Laura Kuenssberg addressed Catherine West and Bridget Phillipson who were sitting waiting for the main interviews. Kuenssberg told West she wanted a cabinet minister to challenge Keir Starmer. She said she was sitting next to one of them. What was her message to her? Well, there’s nothing stopping Bridget from standing. Why are all the men better than the women?

stories.title: Europe should behave more like China does if it wants to survive this age of chaos
stories.summary: Instead of obsessing over rules that have ceased to matter, we must consider giving Beijing a dose of its own medicine The US and Israel may have started the war in Iran, but – apart from the belligerents themselves – it is China and Europe that stand to lose the most from it. Yet while European leaders watch like rabbits caught in the headlights as energy prices shoot through the roof, China has responded to the crisis with remarkable equanimity. It is striking how self-confident Beijing is ahe

stories.title: Report: Iraqi shepherd uncovers alleged secret Israeli base in desert
stories.summary: An intelligence expert said the desert in western Iraq is an ideal location for such secret bases, given its size and sparse population.

stories.title: Oil jumps 4% as Trump rejects Iran’s response to ceasefire proposal
stories.summary: Oil prices jumped on Monday morning while European markets edged lower and Asian stocks rose to new all time highs after US President Donald Trump rejected Tehran’s response to the latest proposal on ending the war in Iran.

stories.title: Putin says Russia will prevail at scaled-down Victory Day parade – video
stories.summary: Russia held its smallest Victory Day parade in years on Saturday due to security concerns over Ukraine drone strikes. The parade on Red Square marks the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany and was once an opportunity to show off Russia's vast military hardware and nuclear missiles, but this year there were no tanks rolling over the cobbles of the Moscow square Russia will always be victorious, says Putin at scaled-back Victory Day parade Continue reading...

stories.title: Greek minister says mystery drone from a 'foreign state'
stories.summary: A Greek military source said the robot, found this week off the island of Lefkada, may have been Ukrainian. Kyiv has admitted using drones to target Russian oil tankers in the Mediterranean.

stories.title: Body of missing US soldier recovered off coast of Morocco
stories.summary: 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr was participating in military exercise among US, Nato allies and African countries A search team recovered the body of a US soldier who went missing near a cliff during a training exercise in Cap Draa, Morocco, the US army said on Sunday. Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1.6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers 'remain asymptomatic' before flights home – video
stories.summary: Spanish health minister Monica Garcia says all those onboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were 'asymptomatic' after it arrived in Tenerife in the Canary Islands on Sunday. Everyone disembarking will be checked for symptoms, authorities say, and be taken off the ship only once evacuation flights are ready to fly them to their destinations Argentina in spotlight over hantavirus as authorities retrace footsteps of ship’s passengers Dozens of people from cruise ship struck by hantavirus leave T

stories.title: Britain and Ireland’s wildflowers – in pictures
stories.summary: The Eden Project’s National Wildflower Centre is opening entries for its Wildflower Photographer of the Year 2026 competition on 29 May. The contest showcases images of some of Britain and Ireland’s 1,600 wildflower species, and a selection of photos from last year’s competition will be on display at Eden Dock, Canary Wharf, London, during CWG’s Nature Week, from 13 July Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: US and Mexico brace for heatwave as deadly floods hit South Africa
stories.summary: Temperatures soar in California and Arizona, while deluge continues across Western and Northern Cape Heat is expected to intensify across western parts of the US and Mexico this week as a ridge of high pressure pushes temperatures well above the seasonal norm. Daytime highs are forecast to reach 10-15C above average in some areas. The US National Weather Service has issued heat advisories for parts of California and Arizona, with extreme heat warnings in force on Monday and Tuesday in places suc

stories.title: Gyurkó Szilvia felel majd a gyermekvédelemért a Szociális és Családügyi Minisztériumnál
stories.summary: A Hintalovon Gyermekjogi Alapítvány alapítója és vezetője április 14-én nyílt levelet is írt az új kormánynak.

stories.title: Ártatlannak vallotta magát a férfi, aki lövöldözni kezdett a fehér házi tudósítók vacsoráján
stories.summary: Cole Allen április végén nyitott tüzet a Fehér Házban.

stories.title: Németh Balázs: Nem szoktam hazudozni
stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezető-helyettese mindenkit fölülmúlt a választók megtévesztésében. Most azt mondta, hogy őt is meglepte, hogy a posztjainak semmi köze nem volt a valósághoz.

stories.title: Kamu katonai behívókat kaptak többen is Dömösön, a rendőrség nyomoz
stories.summary: Zaklatás miatt indított eljárást ismeretlen tettes ellen az Esztergomi Rendőrkapitányság.

stories.title: Videón, ahogy kikísérik a rendőrök Bóna Zoltán volt fideszes országgyűlési képviselőt
stories.summary: A volt fideszes országgyűlési képviselő dunavarsányi otthonában hétfőn reggel volt házkutatás, ezután vitték a Központi Nyomozó Főügyészségre.

stories.title: A TEK és a NIK sorsa még bizonytalan, a titkosszolgálatoknál viszont biztosan lesznek vizsgálatok
stories.summary: A Telex információi szerint a választás óta feszültség volt a TEK állományában, de a hétfői miniszteri meghallgatások alapján még nem dőlt el a szervezet sorsa. A leendő belügyminiszter és a külügyminiszter is arról beszélt, hogy átszervezik a titkosszolgálatok felügyeletét és vizsgálatokat is indítanak.

stories.title: Pénzmosással vádolják Zelenszkij egykori főtanácsadóját
stories.summary: Andrij Jermak azután mondott le tavaly, hogy házkutatást tartottak nála, most úgy tűnik, tényleg lehetett köze az Enerhoatom korrupciós ügyéhez.

stories.title: Az ördög Pradát visel folytatásában a szívet növesztett Miranda Priestly tartja a lelket
stories.summary: Az ördög Pradát viselnek nem volt szüksége folytatásra. Miranda Priestly és a Runway magazin viszont megérdemelte. Vegyes érzésekkel ültem be a moziba, és csak kicsit csalódtam.

stories.title: Berlin: Fighting the city's young far-right scene
stories.summary: A recent police raid on Germany's neo-Nazi scene has revealed how alarmingly young some of its members are. But those targeted by this hatred are fighting back.

stories.title: 'No comment' after Schröder named by Putin for Ukraine talks
stories.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested that the former German chancellor serve as a mediator in Russia-Ukraine peace talks.

stories.title: Germany: 'Ulm 5' on trial after attack on Israeli arms firm
stories.summary: Five activists have gone on trial in Germany following a break-in at the Israeli arms company Elbit Systems in Ulm. Their lawyers intend to argue they were trying to obstruct a genocide.

stories.title: Could German ex-leader really negotiate Ukraine peace?
stories.summary: Former German chancellor Gerhard Schröder is a controversial — and some critics would even say, unserious — choice as a negotiator on the Ukraine war. He's long been criticized for being too close to Russia.

stories.title: Why is Hollywood missing from Cannes?
stories.summary: There are no US blockbusters premiering at the Cannes Film Festival this year. So who's snubbing who?

stories.title: Germany's businesses frustrated by economic decline
stories.summary: No one had higher hopes for Friedrich Merz's chancellorship than the German business community. Now, however, early euphoria has given way to bitter disappointment.

stories.title: Watch: Passengers evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
stories.summary: Passengers from the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius are being evacuated in Tenerife and transferred by bus to quarantine facilities and the airport.

stories.title: Trump rejects Iranian response to US ceasefire proposal as 'totally unacceptable'
stories.summary: Iran had responded to Washington's latest peace proposal earlier in the day, while warning it would not hold back from retaliating against any new US strikes or permit more foreign warships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: UK Army parachutes onto remote Atlantic island over suspected hantavirus case
stories.summary: The British military parachuted medics and emergency supplies into Tristan da Cunha after a resident was suspected of contracting hantavirus.

stories.title: How hotels are stopping the 'dawn dash' for sunbeds after man wins payout
stories.summary: Some resorts enforce allocation rules from check-in to prevent people reserving loungers with towels in the holiday sunbed scramble.

stories.title: His father had just been buried. Then West Bank settlers forced him to dig up the body
stories.summary: The UN human rights office condemns incident as "'appalling and emblematic of the dehumanisation of Palestinians" in the West Bank.

stories.title: Tourist hotspot at 'end of the world' denies causing hantavirus outbreak
stories.summary: The BBC reports from the city of Ushuaia, where experts have been sent to investigate the origins of the outbreak.

stories.title: Iran activists tell BBC how threat of war intensifies trauma of repression
stories.summary: A dissident in Tehran says she feels helpless and under immense psychological pressure.

stories.title: BBC speaks with civilians inside Iran struggling with impact of war
stories.summary: Iranian authorities have imposed an internet ban but using trusted sources, special correspondent Fergal Keane hears from those traumatised by conflict and government repression.

stories.title: Why Eurovision's fallout over Israel may change the competition forever
stories.summary: With Eurovision facing its biggest boycott in 70 years, questions are growing over the competition's future.

stories.title: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
stories.summary: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

stories.title: Trump to discuss Iran war and trade on first US presidential visit to China since 2017
stories.summary: The US leader was expected to travel to China earlier this year, but his trip was postponed due to the Iran war. It marks the first visit by a US president to China since 2017.

stories.title: Venice Biennale performances confront war and climate fears
stories.summary: Artists at the Venice Biennale used striking performances and immersive installations to explore themes including war, caregiving and human fragility.

stories.title: Eurovision 2026 kicks off with turquoise carpet parade in Vienna
stories.summary: Images show large crowds gather to welcome contestants for the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest on the Turquoise Carpet in Vienna.

stories.title: BBC tests out England and Argentina's World Cup training grounds in Kansas City
stories.summary: Will Grant tested out one of the pitches where England captain Harry Kane and his team will call home in Kansas City.

stories.title: US soldier missing in Morocco found dead in Atlantic Ocean
stories.summary: The disappearances triggered a search-and-rescue operation involving more than 600 personnel from the United States, Morocco and other military partners.

stories.title: Iran executes young aerospace engineer over CIA and Mossad espionage allegations
stories.summary: Iran has executed a 29-year-old aerospace engineer Erfan Shakourzadeh on Monday for alleged spying, despite his claims of torture and coerced confession, according to his note from prison.

stories.title: Vienna unveils giant Eurovision mural
stories.summary: Vienna has unveiled Austria's largest mural on a vacant office tower as part of the Eurovision Song Contest cultural programme.
